scenario: Helios SaaS rolls out an internal Camunda 8.8 onboarding form to 200 tenants per cluster. The designer must separate a checkbox that the operator can read but cannot change at runtime from a checkbox the designer turns off only during development for 2 weeks of iteration before the production rollout.
question: Which two checkbox properties separate end-user read-only state from a development-only off state?

options.text: Required handles the end-user case and Hide if handles the development case; Required prevents edits and Hide if removes the box from the form only while designing
Read only makes the checkbox read-only so the user can only read its state, while Disabled disables the checkbox and is documented for use during development
Default value enforces the end-user case by locking the initial state, and Columns is used during development to take the field out of the layout grid
Field label covers the end-user case as a non-editable header, while Field description covers the development case by hiding the field via its description text

a.text: Incorrect. Required enforces submission, not read-only state, and Hide if removes the field at runtime; neither is the documented development-only switch.

b.text: Correct. Read only makes the checkbox read-only at runtime so the user can only read its state, while Disabled is documented as for use during development.

c.text: Incorrect. Default value supplies an initial state and Columns controls layout space; neither is the documented read-only or development toggle.

d.text: Incorrect. Field label and Field description are presentation text, not behavioural switches that lock or disable the checkbox at runtime or design time.

explanation: Read only locks the checkbox so the user can only read its state at runtime, while Disabled is documented as for use during development, giving designers two distinct switches.
